prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of furan computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS, density-fit), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Cation: charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion: charge −1, multiplicity 2. Fukui indices derived via finite-difference Mulliken charge partitioning across the neutral, cation (N−1), and anion (N+1) states. Geometry: user-supplied furan.xyz, single-point (not re-optimized). All three SCFs converged. Integrity: trustworthy=true, all checks passed (Σf⁺=1.0000, Σf⁻=1.0000). No warnings reported. Headline value reported: maximum f⁺ = 1.9242931 at C5 (atom 5, 0-based index 4), identifying it as the most electrophilic site. The most nucleophilic site (highest f⁻ = 0.1616371) is also C5. By symmetry, C4 is essentially equivalent. Dual descriptor confirms C4/C5 as dominant electrophilic centers.
